= Sourdough country loaf
:author: Me
:revdate: 2026-04-14
:tags: favorite, reference

A weekly bake. Open crumb, mahogany crust, mild tang.

[cols="3,1", options="header"]
|===
|Ingredient |Grams
|Bread flour |400
|Whole-wheat flour |100
|Water (84%) |420
|Salt (2.2%) |11
|Active levain |100
|===

== Notes

* Hydration sits at 84%; bumped from 78% after switching flour.
* Bulk ferment ~5h at 24°C with four sets of folds in the first 90 min.
* Flavour is great; link:++recipe://levain-maintenance++[levain schedule] is dialed in.

NOTE: If the dough feels slack at the second fold, hold back 20g of water
on the next bake — easier than coil-folding it back into shape.

== Result

The crumb is open and even. See <<n2,Brown butter chocolate chip cookies>>
for a sweet companion to a slice of this.

TIP: Score with a single deep slash on a slight diagonal — splits open more
predictably than a cross.
